title: host-service-coordinator: real singleton + remove shared-type re-export
description: Fix ISS-007 (getHostServiceCoordinator returns a new HostServiceCoordinator on every call) and ISS-004 (coordinator re-exports shared types from shared/host-info-types, making the shared layer a dead boundary — consumers import types from main layer instead of the shared source).
action: In apps/desktop/src/main/lib/host-service-coordinator.ts: (1) Replace getHostServiceCoordinator body with lazy singleton — add module-level `let instance: HostServiceCoordinator | null = null;` and change the function to `return (instance ??= new HostServiceCoordinator());`. (2) Delete the `export type { HostServiceStatus, HostServiceStatusEvent, Connection, HostServiceStartOpts, HostServiceRestartOpts, HostServiceResetOpts, HostServiceCoordinatorEvents };` re-export block (lines 16-25). Keep the `import type { ... } from "shared/host-info-types"` (lines 6-14) for the class's own type usage. Then redirect the single type consumer: in apps/desktop/src/lib/trpc/routers/host-service-coordinator/index.ts change `import { getHostServiceCoordinator, type HostServiceStatusEvent } from "main/lib/host-service-coordinator"` to two imports — keep `import { getHostServiceCoordinator } from "main/lib/host-service-coordinator"` and add `import type { HostServiceStatusEvent } from "shared/host-info-types"`.

criteria: grep 'let instance: HostServiceCoordinator | null = null' apps/desktop/src/main/lib/host-service-coordinator.ts returns one match
grep 'instance ??= new HostServiceCoordinator' apps/desktop/src/main/lib/host-service-coordinator.ts returns one match
! grep -n 'return new HostServiceCoordinator' apps/desktop/src/main/lib/host-service-coordinator.ts returns no matches
grep -n 'import type { HostServiceStatusEvent } from "shared/host-info-types"' apps/desktop/src/lib/trpc/routers/host-service-coordinator/index.ts returns one match

! grep -n 'type HostServiceStatusEvent' apps/desktop/src/lib/trpc/routers/host-service-coordinator/index.ts | grep 'main/lib/host-service-coordinator' returns no matches (type no longer imported from coordinator)
cd apps/desktop && bun run typecheck exits 0
convergence.verification: Run the grep checks above to confirm singleton pattern + re-export removal + consumer redirect, then `cd apps/desktop && bun run typecheck` to confirm no broken type imports.
convergence.definition_of_done: HostServiceCoordinator is a true process-wide singleton, and shared types are imported only from their shared-layer source — the main-layer coordinator no longer re-exports them.

files.change: Add `let instance: HostServiceCoordinator | null = null;` before the function; change body to `return (instance ??= new HostServiceCoordinator());`; delete the 10-line `export type { ... }` block (lines 16-25) including its `// Re-export types for backward compatibility` comment.

Split `import { getHostServiceCoordinator, type HostServiceStatusEvent } from "main/lib/host-service-coordinator"` into `import { getHostServiceCoordinator } from "main/lib/host-service-coordinator"` and `import type { HostServiceStatusEvent } from "shared/host-info-types"`.

implementation: Step 1: Read host-service-coordinator.ts and the router consumer to confirm current state (re-export block lines 16-25, single type consumer).

Step 2: Edit host-service-coordinator.ts — insert `let instance: HostServiceCoordinator | null = null;` after the class definition (before getHostServiceCoordinator), replace the function body `return new HostServiceCoordinator();` with `return (instance ??= new HostServiceCoordinator());`, and delete the entire `export type { ... };` block + its comment.
Step 3: Edit the router — replace the combined import with split imports (value from coordinator, type from shared/host-info-types).
Step 4: Run `cd apps/desktop && bun run typecheck` — expect exit 0 (no other consumer imports the re-exported types; verified only host-service-coordinator/index.ts imports HostServiceStatusEvent).

reference.pattern: Lazy singleton via `??=` assignment — common Node.js service pattern; matches EventEmitter-based service coordinators.

rationale.chosen_approach: Lazy module-level singleton with `??=` rather than a static class field — keeps the export-as-function API unchanged (backward compatible) and avoids initialization-order issues. Removing the re-export forces consumers to the shared source-of-truth, eliminating the dual-source boundary.
decision_factors: Verified only one type consumer (host-service-coordinator router) imports HostServiceStatusEvent from the re-export.
Runtime-function consumers (getHostId, getHostServiceCoordinator in device/settings/main.full/routers) import values, not types — unaffected.
Existing test file references the class directly, not the singleton — still works.
rationale.tradeoffs: Module-level mutable `let instance` is a process-global; acceptable for an Electron main-process service that should have exactly one coordinator.

risks.risk: A hidden consumer imports a re-exported type not caught by grep
risks.mitigation: typecheck gate catches any broken type import; grep for all 7 re-exported type names across src/ was performed during planning (only HostServiceStatusEvent has a consumer)
